description:
Goal: make Compiler2 own the remaining non-control data shapes that current fixtures treat as ordinary surface: map/struct construction and access, raw bitstrings, and the pattern projections those shapes need.

Signal:
- representative `fz2 run` fixtures that currently fail on `Map`, `Struct`, or `Bitstring` lower and run through Compiler2-native paths
- dispatch-compatible subject projections exist for map values and bitstring fields without reopening old-world lowering

Change:
- add the missing lowered/backend/native/interpreter vocabulary for map/struct/bitstring expressions and projections
- add map/struct/bitstring pattern binding/projection support where control lowering will need it

Why:
- `case` and `receive` support will immediately depend on these shapes, and today Compiler2 rejects them before control selection is even relevant
title: Compiler2 data surface: maps, structs, and bitstrings through the structured pipeline
